purpose: The commit fixes a queuing issue in HCI (Host Controller Interface) for Bluetooth. It ensures that commands are not queued when the HCI is being unregistered, which could lead to use-after-free (UAF) bugs.
The commit removes an unnecessary include statement in the n_hdlc.c file, specifically the asm/termios.h include. This change seems to be related to code cleanup and optimization.

The commit updates documentation for a metric change in the perf c2c tool, specifically adding 'N/A' for store operations. This seems to be a minor clarification or update to existing functionality rather than a new feature or bug fix.

The commit fixes issues related to vma locking in hugetlb, addressing potential bugs in synchronization and sharing of huge pages. It seems to resolve problems that could lead to memory corruption or incorrect behavior in certain scenarios.
The commit adds documentation for the rt5682s codec, specifically for its AVDD and MICVDD supplies. This is a clear and focused change to improve the binding documentation.
The commit fixes a resource leak in the zorro7xx_remove_one() function by adding a missing iounmap() call. This ensures that resources are properly cleaned up when the device is removed.
The commit removes an unused work field from the struct mlx5e_async_ctx, which is a clear and specific technical change.

The commit makes a change to the cfg80211_auth_request struct by making the key_idx member signed. This is done to avoid warnings from static checkers like smatch, as the key_idx can be -1 in some cases.
The commit sorts CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS in RISC-V defconfigs. It does not introduce any new functionality but rearranges configuration options for better organization or consistency.

The commit introduces a new function, mcp251xfd_is_fd_mode(), to check if the chip's FIFOs are configured for CAN-FD mode. This replaces an open-coded check, improving code readability and maintainability.

The commit modifies self-tests for Real-Time Clock (RTC) functionality. It introduces a check to skip tests if an RTC is not present, optimizing test execution by avoiding failures when the RTC is not available.

The commit removes a redundant assignment to the variable 'bytes' in the sound/pci/echoaudio/midi.c file. This change is made to clean up a clang scan build warning, indicating that the value stored in 'bytes' was never actually used.
The commit decouples the presence of rcuscale from RCU Tasks, allowing rcuscale to be built without RCU Tasks. This change aims to reduce kernel size and complexity.
The commit removes duplicated text 'be split' in a header file, specifically in the context of KVM (Kernel-based Virtual Machine) and its memory management.
The commit reverts a change to the clk table population in drm/amd/display due to issues with dcfclk. It aims to prevent underflow until a better solution is implemented.
The commit adds a compatible string for the Renesas RZ/V2L SoC's Mali-G31 GPU, enabling support for this specific hardware configuration.

The commit renames the 'pcie_port' struct to 'dw_pcie_rp' for consistency with other DW PCIe core driver entities. This change seems to be primarily about code organization and naming conventions rather than fixing a bug or adding a new feature.

The commit fixes a warning related to format specifiers in the AMD xgbe driver. It changes the format specifier to match the type of the argument being passed, which is an unsigned short integer. The change is made to avoid a warning about format string mismatches.
The commit fixes a branch range check issue in MIPS jump labels, specifically addressing a negative offset warning by casting the upper bound to long for a signed comparison.
The commit removes a case from SetHwReg8188EU() and directly calls a function, which is part of an ongoing effort to get rid of the unwanted hal layer.
The commit fixes sparse warnings by declaring non-global symbols as static. It addresses a specific issue related to the errata in the arm64 kernel.
The commit removes incorrect interrupt flags from MDSS nodes, fixing a bogus binding issue.

The commit allocates space for arrays in struct ctrl_ref in preparation for allowing drivers to change the array size. It seems to be a preparatory step for future changes rather than fixing an immediate bug.
The commit adds support for new SoCs and PMICs to the socinfo driver, enhancing the kernel's ability to identify and manage these components.
The commit enhances the recognition of chip cuts for the rtl8xxxu wifi driver, extending support beyond the previously known versions (A to E) to all possible versions (A to P).
The commit removes custom software enable count for PRCMU clocks, relying on the core's software enable count instead.

The commit aims to refactor the code to reduce the usage of #ifdef CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL by grouping related definitions together. It seems to be a code cleanup effort rather than fixing a specific bug or adding a new feature.
The commit enables QUP and GPI DMA hardware to add functioning I2C nodes later. It seems to be a preparatory change to allow for additional functionality in the future.

The commit fixes comments and typos in the thermal driver code. It corrects the description of 'Capture times' to 'Total number of ADC data samples', fixes a typo in 'caliberation' to 'calibration', and adjusts coding style for /* comments.
The commit fixes an infinite loop issue in the igc driver, which occurs when the HW semaphore cannot be acquired during resource release. This typically happens when the hardware is surprise-removed.

The commit updates the pcm3168a driver to use modern ASoC DAI format terminology, specifically changing how bus clocks are defined and used. This seems to be a code refactoring effort to align with newer standards or practices in the ASoC (Advanced System on Chip) architecture.
The commit removes a pointless IS_SWAPFILE check, as it is always defined. This change simplifies the code by eliminating an unnecessary conditional.
The commit implements GTP echo response, which is a feature that was missing. It adds the capability to respond to GTP echo requests, which is necessary for GTP compliance.
The commit replaces cpumask_next_* with cpumask_first_* where appropriate, improving the effectiveness of cpumask operations. It also introduces cpumask_first_zero() to handle a specific case.

The commit removes a call to 'dtim_assoc' IOVAR, which was failing and causing a message to be displayed on the console every time a roam operation was completed. The change ensures that the host driver no longer attempts to get TIM information from the firmware, as this is no longer supported.

The commit adds support for a platform without an amplifier, specifically for the alc5682 headset codec. It introduces a new board configuration, adl_rt5682, and connects it to SSP0, following Intel's BT offload design.
The commit fixes an issue with zerocopy sendmsg for protocols that do not support it. It ensures that when a socket does not support zerocopy, the sendmsg operation will fail.
The commit improves the handling of xeCJK.sty in kerneldoc-preamble by testing its existence before loading, which helps in avoiding errors on certain Linux distributions.
The commit allows certain macros to be used without reporting errors, specifically 'case' macros in the kernel. It seems to be relaxing the check for complex values in macros.
The commit replaces simple-KMS helpers with regular atomic helpers for drm/simpledrm, improving code sharing and architecture.
The commit adds a jpeg decoding worker interface to ensure that three HWs run in parallel in MT8195. This suggests a new feature or enhancement to the existing media processing capabilities.
The commit removes unnecessary VPRINTK() calls and ata_scsi_dump_cdb() as they are redundant with existing tracepoints.

The commit adds support for zstate during extended vblank in the drm/amd/display module. It allows the system to enter a low-power state (zstate) when in FREESYNC mode, potentially improving power efficiency. The change involves updating the vblank and calculating optimized values for min_dst_y_next_start.
The commit fixes a type mismatch between the expected and actual types of the reset_type parameter in the hqd_destroy() callback function, which was causing a Control Flow Integrity (CFI) failure.
The commit corrects the 'resets' property name in the pinctrl binding for stm32. It also adds a missing 'additionalProperties' constraint.

The commit addresses an array-bounds warning in the ath9k driver for GCC 12. It seems to relegate or suppress the warning rather than fixing the underlying issue, as the warning is related to how the code accesses an array.
The commit replaces pm_runtime_get_sync with pm_runtime_resume_and_get, simplifying the code.

The commit reorganizes and refines the installation and removal of fixed files in the io_uring context. It aims to put the related code in a more appropriate place, specifically with the filetable code, and adjusts the helper functions to operate on the context rather than directly on requests. This reorganization seems to improve code structure and coherence.
The commit updates device bindings to support separate nodes for G1 and G2 decoders, enabling independent control via the vpu-blk-ctrl power-domain controller.
The commit removes a macro (IWL_DEVICE_BZ_COMMON) that was only used once inside another macro (IWL_DEVICE_BZ). It simplifies the code by directly incorporating the contents of the removed macro.
